message:
build(makefile): add automatic header dependency tracking with -MMD -MP

Enable GCC/Clang automatic dependency file generation so that changes
to any included header trigger recompilation of the affected .o files.

- Add DEP_FLAGS := -MMD -MP to the flag definitions.
- Pass $(DEP_FLAGS) in the src/%.c -> $(OBJ_DIR)/%.o pattern rule.
- Compute LIB_DEP from LIB_OBJ (.o -> .d) and include them via -include.

The -MMD flag generates .d dependency files as a side effect of
compilation, and -MP adds phony targets for each dependency so that
deleted headers do not cause make errors.  The -include directive
silently ignores missing .d files on clean builds.

This eliminates a class of incremental-build staleness bugs where
editing a header (e.g., adding a struct field to cleanup.h) would not
trigger recompilation of source files that include it, leading to ODR
violations or silent ABI mismatches in the resulting libasx.a.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -90,6 +90,7 @@ WARN_FLAGS := -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Werror \
 
 # C standard
 STD_FLAGS := -std=c99
+DEP_FLAGS := -MMD -MP
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Include paths
@@ -168,6 +169,7 @@ BIN_DIR   := $(BUILD_DIR)/bin
 TEST_DIR  := $(BUILD_DIR)/tests
 
 LIB_OBJ := $(patsubst src/%.c,$(OBJ_DIR)/%.o,$(LIB_SRC))
+LIB_DEP := $(LIB_OBJ:.o=.d)
 LIB_A   := $(LIB_DIR)/libasx.a
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@@ -258,7 +260,9 @@ $(LIB_A): $(LIB_OBJ) | $(LIB_DIR)
 	$(AR) rcs $@ $^
 
 $(OBJ_DIR)/%.o: src/%.c | obj-dirs
-	$(CC) $(ALL_CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
+	$(CC) $(ALL_CFLAGS) $(DEP_FLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
+
+-include $(LIB_DEP)
 
 obj-dirs:
 	@mkdir -p $(OBJ_DIR)/core $(OBJ_DIR)/runtime $(OBJ_DIR)/channel \
